item.title: Version 0.9.17.0
item.description:
• Speculative fix for a launch crash on macOS 14.x: the `Array.indexed(by:)` helper and the Settings Appearance / Menu bar display pickers now take closures instead of generic-context KeyPaths, which avoids a buggy KeyPath caching path in older Sonoma builds.
  • Reorder the Settings cards: About → Account → Preferences → Favorites, so the most-glanced content is at the top and the long-tail Favorites list is at the bottom.
  • Stability + observability work under the hood: cross-midnight running-timer detection, automatic timezone-change handling, hardened sign-out (clears all in-memory state), graceful notification-permission degradation with a Settings warning + System Settings deep link, a local rolling log at ~/Library/Logs/Yield/yield.log, and pre-filled GitHub bug reports that include version / OS / last refresh / last error.
  • Test coverage: 92 unit tests now cover date helpers, hour formatters, project status thresholds, the refresh merge pipeline, optimistic timer mutations, and the budget-notification gate. CI runs them on every push.

item.title: Version 0.9.16.1
item.description:
• Fix the new/edit timer popup clipping its top edge (or hovering low) when picking from the Favorites list with a duplicate-entry banner involved. The favorites picker now uses the same native dropdown machinery as the project / task pickers, so the popup resizes cleanly. Visual styling is preserved — leading star, two-line "Project / Task" layout, dividers between items.
  • Sort the favorites list alphabetically by client → project → task to match the project list (was sorted by most-recently-used).
  • Match the new/edit timer notes field's vertical padding to its horizontal padding so the first line doesn't run along the top border.

item.title: Version 0.9.16.0
item.description:
• Favorites. Save a project + task combo as a favorite from the new/edit timer screen by tapping the star to the right of the pickers. Picking a project auto-selects its most-recently-used favorite task, and favorited tasks light up with a star inside the task dropdown so they're easy to spot. A new "★ Favorites ▾" button next to the project picker opens a popover for one-tap selection of a saved combo, and a Favorites card in Settings lists every favorite with a remove button.
  • Day-filter only shows days you tracked. Booked projects with no time on the filtered day are now hidden — the day-filter view answers "what did I work on this day" rather than "everything booked plus what I worked on".
  • Fix Sunday's logged hours showing up Monday morning when the app stayed open across a sleep — the cache now invalidates on Mac wake and on day rollover at panel-open, regardless of whether the 60s background-refresh timer fires reliably.
  • Fix the Time's up! notification firing when a timer starts on a project that's already over budget. It now only fires when this tracking session's elapsed time crosses the booking.
  • Trim the popup window so it sizes to its content — short project lists no longer leave a gap between the timer banner and the projects, and the new/edit timer view no longer pads itself below the form.
  • Layout polish: drawer time entries sort newest-day-first (no more running-first hoist), the project list / footer divider no longer renders as a doubled border, the "Recent" tab is renamed to "All", and dropdown text is vertically centered.
  • Notes field gets balanced top/bottom padding so the first line doesn't run along the top border.

item.title: Version 0.9.15.0
item.description:
• Timer-change HUD. When the running Harvest timer changes from outside Yield (e.g. you start a timer from the browser extension), a small translucent panel pops below the Yield menu bar icon for 8 seconds with a Started / Stopped label, the project, and the task. The HUD stays silent — and dismisses immediately — when the main popup is open, since you're already looking at the timer state.
  • Optimistic timer toggles. Start / stop buttons (in project rows, the drawer, and the active-timer banner) flip the UI immediately and run the Harvest API call in the background. The next refresh reconciles, and a failed API call refreshes from the catch branch to undo the optimistic mutation.
  • Day-filter only shows days you tracked. Booked projects with no time on the filtered day are now hidden — the day-filter view reads as "what did I work on this day" instead of "everything booked plus what I worked on".
  • Fix the Time's up! notification firing when a timer starts on a project that's already over budget. The notification now only fires when this tracking session's elapsed time crosses the booking — not when a timer starts already-over (via the UI, an external Harvest action, or app launch with a pre-existing running timer).
  • Drawer time entries sort newest-day-first; the active timer no longer hoists itself to the top of the list — it sits naturally within today's entries.

item.title: Version 0.9.14.0
item.description: • Light mode. Settings now has an Appearance picker with System / Light / Dark options. The light palette was tuned for WCAG AA contrast (≥ 4.5:1) — green, yellow, over, and prospective accents shift to darker variants on the light background while keeping their dark-mode brightness intact. New installs default to Dark.

item.title: Version 0.9.13.2
item.description:
• Move idle time to another timer. The idle alert now offers a fourth option — "Move Time…" — that opens the new-timer form prefilled with the captured idle minutes. Pick a different project/task and commit; the idle minutes are removed from the original timer and added to the destination. If the destination already has an entry today, the duplicate banner offers "Add to existing" so the time is merged into the existing entry instead of creating a duplicate. The source timer isn't touched until the destination commit succeeds, so cancelling out is a no-op on data.
  • Day-filter row reading. When a weekday filter is active, each project row's time label switches from "week / booked" to "day / week-so-far" — the day's contribution framed against your week-to-date total — and the progress bar layers a full-opacity day overlay on top of a 40%-dimmed week fill so you can read both at once.
  • Menu bar icon tooltip. Hovering the menu bar icon while a timer is running shows "Client — Project" as a system tooltip, matching the active-timer banner's labeling.

item.title: Version 0.9.13.1
item.description:
• Save / Start / Log dismiss the form immediately. The new/edit timer form now closes right away when you hit Save (or Start Timer / Log Time / Resume Existing); the API round-trip continues in the background and the panel header's existing progress indicator pulses to show work is happening. No more dead moment staring at a disabled form.
  • Fix editing an existing entry pre-filling 0:00 instead of the entry's actual time — a regression from the v0.9.13.0 simplify pass that dropped the input field's binding observers. Restored.
  • Fix saving an edited time silently dropping the change when you hit Save with the time field still focused. The single-field input now updates the bindings live as you type, so Save sees the latest value even without first tabbing out.
  • Fix unbooked projects (e.g. PTO) silently disappearing on day rollover when a weekday filter from the previous day was still active. The filter now auto-clears at the start of a new day.

item.title: Version 0.9.13.0
item.description:
• Single-field time entry — the new/edit time form's HH:MM input is now one field instead of two. Type 1:30 or 1.5 (or 1,5) and either lands as 1h 30m. Reformats to H:MM on commit. Larger 16pt mono font for readability.
  • Double-click the active timer banner to edit — same destination as the right-click "Edit Timer" menu, matches the existing double-click pattern in the project drawers.
  • Active timer banner shows the client name — top label is now CLIENT — PROJECT when a client is known, so projects with shared names across clients are no longer ambiguous. Em-dash matches the convention in the over-budget notification and idle alert.
  • Smoother timer banner animations — the empty strip and active banner are now one persistent component that grows in place when a timer starts and collapses when it stops. The status dot pulses gently while running to convey "live" peripherally. Resume no longer flickers the banner off and on. The whole transition reads as one row growing rather than two components handing off.
  • Project list is now alphabetical by client → project across the whole list. Booked and logged-only projects intermingle so the list reads as one alphabetical sweep instead of two visually separate groups.
  • Pause no longer jumps the banner up by any other entries on the same project earlier in the week. Pausing freezes the running entry's elapsed, not the project's week total.
  • HH:MM displays now match Harvest's rounding — Yield was truncating, so 3.51h could read as 3:30 while Harvest showed 3:31. Now rounds to nearest minute everywhere (row totals, banner, menu bar label).

item.title: Version 0.9.12.0
item.description:
• Smarter API outage messages — when Harvest or Forecast fails, Yield now checks harveststatus.com and tells you what's actually going on. If there's a confirmed incident you'll see its name, how long ago it started, and a link to the status page; if the status page reports no issues you'll see a hint that the failure is more likely on your end (connection, sign-in).
  • Long project lists now scroll — when there are more projects than fit on screen, the panel caps just under the screen height and the project list scrolls inside while the header, banners, time-off row, timer banner, and footer stay pinned. The panel still sizes naturally when content fits.
  • Multi-display: the panel correctly re-sizes for the screen it's actually on when you click the menu bar icon on a different display.

item.title: Version 0.9.11.0
item.description:
• Menu bar display setting — pick what shows next to the icon: Project time (the original tracked-vs-booked-for-the-week behavior), Day time (current timer vs. today's running total), or Current timer (just the running timer's hours, in a narrower menu bar item). Configurable in Settings → Preferences.
  • Fix Forecast PTO disappearing on a new week while booked tasks looked correct — the Time Off project ID lookup now falls back to the last-known-good cached value when /projects returns an unexpectedly incomplete response.
  • Fix stale day-dependent Forecast state when the app is open across midnight within the same week — the soft refresh now also detects day rollover (not just week rollover) and falls back to a hard refresh.
  • Project drawer auto-collapses when its last visible time entry is deleted, instead of getting stuck open with no content.

item.title: Version 0.9.10.5
item.description: • Fix tall Forecast notes tooltip on macOS 14 (Sonoma) — the SwiftUI popover-sizing combo we were using triggered a layout bug specific to that OS, rendering the tooltip absurdly tall regardless of content. macOS 14 now uses a deterministic fixed-width layout; macOS 15+ keeps the original fluid layout that hugs short notes and wraps long ones.

item.title: Version 0.9.10.4
item.description: • Second pass at the tall-tooltip bug — 0.9.10.3's normalization missed the specific invisible characters actually showing up in one Forecast account. Switched to Unicode's standard "default-ignorable" classification (catches every variation selector, bidi mark, tag character, and formatting code). Also deduped identical notes across multiple assignments, and capped the tooltip at 30 lines as a safety net.

item.title: Version 0.9.10.3
item.description: • Fix Forecast notes tooltip rendering as a very tall box with a single line of text floating inside — internal blank lines in a note now collapse to at most one, and leading/trailing blanks get stripped.

item.title: Version 0.9.10.2
item.description:
• Fix refresh wiping the viewed past/future week — a menu-open or manual refresh while browsing another week was flashing the UI to current-week data. The viewed week's snapshot is now preserved through the refresh and a fresh fetch for that week runs alongside the current-week refresh.
  • Faster, nicer Forecast notes tooltip — hovering the notes icon now pops open in ~100ms (the native macOS tooltip delay was ~1s and not tunable), positioned to the right of the icon with larger text, line spacing, and padding.

item.title: Version 0.9.10.1
item.description: • Fix the Forecast notes icon not rendering on macOS 14 (Sonoma) — it was using an SF Symbol that only shipped in macOS 15.

item.title: Version 0.9.10.0
item.description:
• Day-of-week filter + look-ahead — filter the project list by day of the week to see just what's booked (or tracked) on that day. A new look-ahead section surfaces expected hours, company holidays, and prospective bookings for the days still ahead this week.
  • Add time to any day of the current week — the new-timer form now lets you target any day in the current week, not just today.
  • Forecast assignment notes on project rows — notes attached to a Forecast assignment now surface on the matching project row with a notes icon and tooltip.
  • Projects sort by client then name so the order stays stable between refreshes.
  • Renamed the project context menu's "Start Timer" to "Add Time" for clarity.
  • Row header stays week-level when you narrow to a single day, so the totals don't collapse out from under you.
  • Under the hood: deduped fetches, pre-computed week state, and the Time Off project ID now persists across launches to avoid a second fetch on refresh.

item.title: Version 0.9.9.0
item.description:
• Look ahead and look back — new chevron controls next to the week label let you step forward and backward through weeks. Past weeks show what you tracked (read-only, with drawers and the day-by-day bar); future weeks show what's booked in Forecast. A "This Week" pill returns you to the current week.
  • Tighter network dropout handling — 20-second request timeout so airplane mode and captive portals fail fast, a reachability listener that refreshes the moment the machine reconnects, and exponential backoff so long outages don't hammer the API. The menu bar icon and Time Off row now also react to connectivity loss.
  • Week label uses a compact Americanized format: "Apr 20 – 26, 2026" same-month, "Apr 27 – May 3, 2026" cross-month, "Dec 29, 2025 – Jan 4, 2026" cross-year.
  • Time Off row formats as days + hours + minutes (8h = 1d), skipping zero components: "3d", "1d 4h", "4h 30m".
  • New/Edit time entry header reads "Today, Apr 21" when the spent date is today.
  • "Forecasted" tab renamed to "Booked" and the panel widened to 540pt to give the week label breathing room alongside the tab toggle.
  • Smoother week-to-week transitions — content cross-fades instead of popping when you navigate.
  • Under the hood: parallelized fetches, cached person/user IDs, cleaned-up view-model encapsulation, and a codebase-wide quality pass.

item.title: Version 0.9.8.4
item.description:
• Day-by-day segmented bar — expanding a project's drawer now shows a full-width bar at the top broken into per-day segments, with each segment sized proportionally to that day's hours. Today's segment is at full brightness; past days are slightly dimmed. Animates in when the drawer opens. Hover a segment for a tooltip like "Mon: 3:15".
  • Double-click to edit — double-clicking a time entry in a project drawer opens the edit form, as a quicker alternative to the right-click menu.
  • Moved the Time Off summary row to the very top of the panel (above the active timer banner) so it's always visible without scrolling.

item.title: Version 0.9.8.3
item.description:
• Time Off awareness — a new read-only row at the bottom of the project list surfaces Forecast time-off bookings for the current week (affected days + total hours)
  • Out-of-office menu bar icon — on a full-day PTO day with no timer running, the menu bar icon changes to 🌙💤 so you know the app knows you're off
  • Time entries inside a project drawer now sort Monday → Friday (oldest first) instead of Friday → Monday
  • New/Edit time entry header now shows the weekday: "Mon, Apr 20" instead of just "Apr 20"
  • Fixed collapsed project drawers swallowing right-clicks intended for rows below them

item.title: Version 0.9.8.2
item.description:
• Duplicate timer guard — when starting a timer on a project + task that already has time logged today, a confirmation banner lets you resume the existing entry or create a new one
  • PNG chart export — save your weekly time chart as a PNG image via the download button in the chart header
  • Smoother animations — project task lists expand with an accordion drawer instead of fading in; timer banner no longer flashes when switching to the chart tab
  • Fixed idle detection — now tracks keyboard, clicks, and scroll activity (previously only tracked mouse movement due to a Swift enum bridging bug)
  • Fixed duplicate over-budget notifications when stopping and restarting a timer on a project already over its hours
  • Internal refactoring: consolidated API client, reduced redundant chart computations, merged week-tracking state

item.title: Version 0.9.8.1
item.description:
• Click a project in the chart legend to isolate it — the chart dims the other projects so you can focus on just one. Click it again to return to the full stack.
  • Chart defaults to Mon–Fri and only extends into Sat/Sun when time was tracked on those days
  • Chart now runs edge-to-edge within its panel (no more wasted horizontal padding at the Mon/Sun edges)
  • Y-axis stays pinned to the week's true peak when isolating, so the chart doesn't visually shrink

item.title: Version 0.9.8.0
item.description:
• New weekly time chart tab in the header — stacked area chart showing each project's contribution to your daily totals across the week
  • Y-axis starts at 0–8h with a dashed 8h workday reference line, growing in 2h steps if you log a longer day
  • Colors are auto-computed to be maximally distinct for however many projects you have, tuned as muted tones for the dark theme

item.title: Version 0.9.7.6
item.description:
• Start Timer with pre-filled hours now actually starts a running timer instead of logging a stopped entry
  • The entire visible dropdown area is clickable — no more dead strips in the padding
  • Client names in the project dropdown are no longer selectable; only projects underneath them are

item.title: Version 0.9.7.5
item.description:
• Opening the menu bar now triggers a fresh refresh, so timers started outside the app show up right away
  • Added a loading spinner next to the week label during refreshes
  • New 1-minute soft refresh keeps today's timer state near-real-time without hammering the API
  • Active timer banner now shows HH:MM with a flashing colon — no more seconds snapping backward on refresh
  • Pre-fill hours when starting a new timer to catch up on time you forgot to track

item.title: Version 0.9.7.4
item.description:
• Fix task list not populating when adding time — non-admin Harvest users can now see and select tasks
  • Weekday hover "+" and Settings back button now use white instead of green

item.title: Version 0.9.7.3
item.description:
• Click any day in the week header to add a time entry to that day — useful for catching up or pre-logging PTO
  • Hover any weekday to see a green "+" affordance, with an "Add Time" tooltip
  • The new entry form title reflects the target date (e.g. "New time entry: Apr 11")
  • Start Timer is only offered when the target day is today; other days log time directly

item.title: Version 0.9.7.2
item.description:
• Menu bar label now shows a "tracked / budget" pair — running entry vs. today's total for unbooked timers, project vs. booked for booked timers, weekly total vs. forecast when idle
  • Gauge needle recalibrated to sweep a full 270° from lower-left (0%) through the top (50%) to lower-right (100%)
  • Right-click the active timer banner to edit or delete the current entry
  • No duplicate over-budget notification when starting a timer on a project that's already over

item.title: Version 0.9.7.1
item.description:
• Menu bar now uses distinct icons to convey state: calendar (no timer), gauge (booked timer), timer (unbooked), warning triangle (API error)
  • Time labels are tighter — minus prefix for over-budget (e.g. -8:10) instead of "left"/"over" suffix
  • Paused timers keep showing their project's state in the menu bar (without the active dot)

item.title: Version 0.9.7
item.description:
• Daily hours breakdown in header: Mon–Sun totals with week total
  • Current day highlighted with brighter text
  • Clock icon next to today when a timer is running

item.title: Version 0.9.6.5
item.description:
• Remaining label switches to "Xh XXm over this week" in red when over budget
  • Detect Harvest/Forecast API outages with per-service warning banner
  • Menu bar icon switches to warning triangle during API errors
  • Timer controls disabled when Harvest is unreachable

item.title: Version 0.9.6.4
item.description:
• Progress bar now shows overage: turns red when logged hours exceed booked
  • Simplified status colors: green under 100%, red over 100%
  • New accent red (#F05D28)

item.title: Version 0.9.6.3
item.description:
• Add bug report button in footer (opens GitHub Issues)
  • Add automated release skill

item.title: Version 0.9.6.2
item.description:
• Fix DELETE endpoint crash when deleting time entries
  • Fix OAuth token refresh race condition
  • Move OAuth secrets out of source code into build config
  • Eliminate all force-unwraps for crash safety
  • Fix timer display after app backgrounding
  • Fix pagination edge cases in API calls
  • Fix notes being cleared when editing time entries
  • Add thread safety to keychain cache
  • Prevent ID collisions between Forecast and Harvest projects

item.title: Version 0.9.6.1
item.description:
• Tab toggle matches Figma: opaque colors, no inner borders
  • Inactive timer slot shows subtle green gradient bar below header
  • Menu bar gauge reflects overall weekly progress when no timer is running
  • Improved Slack release notification formatting

item.title: Version 0.9.6
item.description:
• New timer entry form: create, edit, and delete time entries with project/task dropdowns grouped by client
  • Idle detection alert: full-screen takeover with continue, stop, or keep options
  • Settings moved inline into menu bar panel with card-based UI
  • Hover states on all buttons, project rows, and interactive elements
  • Smooth transitions between views (slide, fade, cross-fade)
  • Animated progress bars and tab toggle
  • Forecasted project indicator: white left-border line

item.title: Version 0.9.5.1
item.description:
• Menu bar icon now adapts to wallpaper and system appearance (template image)
  • Updated app icon
  • "Today" label for current day time entries

item.title: Version 0.9.5
item.description:
• UI redesign: new header with Recent/Forecasted tabs, redesigned project rows with status lines and progress bars
  • Timer banner: pause/resume/stop active timers with live HH:MM:SS display
  • Paused timer state with yellow/amber visual indicator
  • Menu bar: new gauge icon with rotation based on progress, fixed-width layout
  • Expandable project rows with smooth animation and refined time entry list
  • Custom typography: Newsreader, DM Sans, JetBrains Mono

item.title: Version 0.9.4.1
item.description:
• Fix OAuth sign-in not completing after fresh install
  • Fetch Harvest and Forecast account IDs from API when not included in OAuth scope
  • Fix Keychain storage to fall back to legacy keychain when Data Protection is unavailable

item.title: Version 0.9.4
item.description:
• Force dark mode for consistent appearance
  • Add appearance picker in Settings (coming soon: light and system modes)
  • Remove legacy personal access token authentication — OAuth only
  • Fix opaque menu bar panel background

item.title: Version 0.9.3
item.description:
• Fix version number display in gear menu (was showing v1.0 instead of actual version)
  • Info.plist now uses build setting variables for version — single source of truth in project.yml

item.title: Version 0.9.2
item.description:
• Fix Keychain prompt on app updates — no more password dialog after updating
  • Version number now correctly displayed in gear menu
  • Daily automatic update checks via Sparkle

item.title: Version 0.9.1
item.description:
• Fixed OAuth "server not found" error on sign-in
  • Settings now display inline in the menu bar panel
  • Refresh, Settings, Updates, and Quit in gear dropdown
  • Today's total hours shown in top bar
  • Times shown as h:mm format
  • Signed and notarized by Apple
